openmeter/billing/rating/service.purpose: Sole stateless implementation of rating.Service: GenerateDetailedLines dispatches a billable line to the correct per-price-type Pricer (in rate/) wrapped by an ordered pre/post mutator pipeline (in mutator/), then aggregates currency-rounded totals. The service struct holds no fields; all state flows through method arguments.

patterns.name: getPricerFor() is the only pricer/mutator assembly point
patterns.description: Never instantiate rate.Flat{}/rate.Unit{}/rate.Tiered{} etc. directly; getPricerFor(line, opts) selects the base Pricer and builds the ordered PreCalculation/PostCalculation mutator slices by price type and options.

patterns.name: No Usage for FlatPriceType lines
patterns.description: input.Usage stays nil for productcatalog.FlatPriceType; non-flat lines must populate it from GetMeteredQuantity()/GetMeteredPreLinePeriodQuantity() before calling the pricer, or PricerCalculateInput.Validate() errors.

patterns.name: Totals aggregated only in getTotalsFromDetailedLines (service layer)
patterns.description: Pricers and mutators stay currency-agnostic; after priceMutator.GenerateDetailedLines, the service calls getTotalsFromDetailedLines(out, currencyCalc) which sums per-line totals via totals.Sum and is the only aggregate-level rounding point.

patterns.name: currencyx.Calculator for all rounding
patterns.description: Resolve the calculator once via in.GetCurrency().Calculator() and thread it through PricerCalculateInput; use calc.RoundToPrecision() for every amount — never assign raw alpacadecimal arithmetic to a total.

patterns.name: validateStandardLine before any pricer call
patterns.description: GenerateDetailedLines and ResolveBillablePeriod call validateStandardLine(in) first: line non-nil, price non-nil, and a non-progressively-billed line's full service period equals its progressively-billed service period.
patterns.example: if err := validateStandardLine(in); err != nil { return ..., fmt.Errorf("validating billable line: %w", err) }

patterns.name: ProgressiveBilling gated on monotonic meter aggregation
patterns.description: ResolveBillablePeriod forces in.ProgressiveBilling=false unless isDependingOnIncreaseOnlyMeters returns true (only Sum/Count/Max/UniqueCount); other aggregations (Avg, Latest) bill in arrears.

patterns.name: Option flags gate specific mutators in getPricerFor
patterns.description: opts.IgnoreMinimumCommitment suppresses MinAmountCommitment (used by charges/partial runs); opts.DisableCreditsMutator suppresses Credits; MaxAmountCommitment is always appended for non-flat lines.

key_file_guides.role: Zero-field service struct and New() — the sole DI entry point for rating.Service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: service has no fields; adding any breaks the stateless design and risks shared state across concurrent billing runs.

key_file_guides.role: getPricerFor() maps price types to rate.Pricer + ordered mutators; priceMutator orchestrates pre/post pipeline and delegates ResolveBillablePeriod to the inner Pricer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new price type needs a new switch case; missing case returns an unsupported-price-type error at runtime. Ensure DisableCreditsMutator/IgnoreMinimumCommitment propagate.

key_file_guides.role: GenerateDetailedLines entry: validates, resolves calculator, builds PricerCalculateInput, runs priceMutator, aggregates via getTotalsFromDetailedLines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not set input.Usage for FlatPriceType; never skip getTotalsFromDetailedLines; CategoryCommitment lines accumulate in ChargesTotal, not Amount.

key_file_guides.role: ResolveBillablePeriod gates ProgressiveBilling via isDependingOnIncreaseOnlyMeters before delegating to the matched pricer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returning true for a non-monotonic aggregation (Avg, Latest) causes incorrect progressive billing — only Sum/Count/Max/UniqueCount qualify.

key_file_guides.role: Demonstrates testutil.RunCalculationTestCase for option-level tests (IgnoreMinimumCommitment, DisableCreditsMutator).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All pricer/mutator tests go through testutil.RunCalculationTestCase; do not instantiate service.New() directly in tests.

anti_patterns: Instantiating rate.Unit{}/rate.Flat{}/rate.Tiered{} directly instead of going through getPricerFor()
Setting input.Usage for FlatPriceType lines — PricerCalculateInput.Validate() errors
Computing DetailedLine totals inside a pricer or mutator instead of getTotalsFromDetailedLines
Adding fields/state to the service struct — it is intentionally stateless
Raw alpacadecimal arithmetic for final amounts without currencyx.Calculator.RoundToPrecision

decisions.decision: priceMutator wraps a single Pricer with ordered pre/post mutator slices instead of embedding mutation in each Pricer
decisions.rationale: Keeps pricing concerns (tier math, period gating) separate from cross-cutting concerns (discounts, commitments, credits) so each evolves and tests in isolation.

decisions.decision: service struct has no fields; New() returns an interface over an empty struct
decisions.rationale: Rating is purely computational with no pools/caches/config; statelessness simplifies testing and avoids shared state across concurrent billing runs.

decisions.decision: Totals aggregated at the service layer, not inside priceMutator
decisions.rationale: Aggregation depends on the currency calculator resolved at the service layer; pricers/mutators stay currency-agnostic and independently testable.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new price type: register in getPricerFor() and implement rate.Pricer
